title: Container with systemd running inside crashes silently with --userns=keep-id:uid=10000,gid=10000
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**

/kind bug

Hi. Merry Christmas !


**Description**

I'm using rootless podman.

I wanted systemd in a container like https://developers.redhat.com/blog/2019/04/24/how-to-run-systemd-in-a-container

I have a user with UID, GID 10000 inside my container. It works when I use the Containerfile below.

However it crashes when I run the container with `--userns=keep-id:uid=10000,gid=10000` 
I want to do this so that my files shared via a volume have the same owner both inside and outside the container.

Containerfile 

```
FROM fedora


RUN dnf -y install sudo systemd util-linux ansible ansible-core

RUN groupadd -g 10000 icewreck
RUN useradd -m -d /home/icewreck -s /bin/bash -g 10000 -G wheel -u 10000 icewreck
RUN echo '%wheel ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL' >> /etc/sudoers

# I think USER wont work here because this sets the user for ENTRYPOINT and CMD

CMD [ "/usr/sbin/init" ]
```

And then `podman build -t icewreck/devenv .`


This works:

```
podman run -d \
    --name testdev \
    icewreck/devenv

podman exec -ti testdev /bin/bash
```

But this doesn't

```
podman run -d \
    --userns=keep-id:uid=10000,gid=10000 \
    --name testdev \
    icewreck/devenv

podman exec -ti testdev /bin/bash
```



**Describe the results you received:**

The build succeeds but the container dies on creation.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Container works just like before.



**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.19.2
Built:        Fri Nov 11 20:31:27 2022

```

Yes

comments.body:
I kind of achieved what I wanted with s6-overlay service manager instead of systemd. Not ideal but it works.  Still, would be nice to figure out what went wrong with systemd.

Another question if you have time. 
Here's the situation:
- rootless podman container started with `userns=keep-id:uid=10000,gid=10000`,
- user account called xyz inside container with uid,gid=10000 (manually added with useradd; not using Containerfile's USER directive)
- a service was running as root (uid 0) inside that container
- tried to drop its privileges and run it as uid =10000, gid = 10000

This worked. However when it tried to set the process to run as xyz's  supplementary group list it failed.  What is the reason behind that ? I figure its got something to do with linux namespaces and how podman is mapping them with userns=keep-id but i dont know enough to figure it out.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

when you use `--userns=keep-id` it also internally sets the UID and GID for the container process.

systemd needs to run as root, so you've to override it with `--user 0:0`:

```
podman run -d \
    --userns=keep-id:uid=10000,gid=10000 --user 0:0 \
    --name testdev \
    icewreck/devenv
```
